name: ruamel-yaml-clib

metadata.name: ruamel.yaml.clib
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: C version of reader, parser and emitter for ruamel.yaml derived from libyaml

documentation:
# ruamel.yaml.clib `ruamel.yaml.clib` is the C based reader/scanner and emitter for ruamel.yaml
version 0.2.12
updated 2024-10-20
documentation
repository
pypi
This package was split of from ruamel.yaml, so that ruamel.yaml can be build as a universal wheel. Apart from the C code seldom changing, and taking a long time to compile for all platforms, this allows installation of the .so on Linux systems under /usr/lib64/pythonX.Y (without a .pth file or a ruamel directory) and the Python code for ruamel.yaml under /usr/lib/pythonX.Y. [![image](https://bestpractices.coreinfrastructure.org/projects/1128/badge)](https://bestpractices.coreinfrastructure.org/projects/1128) [![image](https://sourceforge.net/p/ruamel-yaml-clib/code/ci/default/tree/_doc/_static/license.svg?format=raw)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) [![image](https://sourceforge.net/p/ruamel-yaml-clib/code/ci/default/tree/_doc/_static/pypi.svg?format=raw)](https://pypi.org/project/ruamel.yaml.clib/) This release in loving memory of Johanna Clasina van der Neut-Bandel \[1922-10-19 – 2015-11-21\]

functions.name: setup.literal_eval
documentation:
Safely evaluate an expression node or a string containing a Python
expression.  The string or node provided may only consist of the following
Python literal structures: strings, bytes, numbers, tuples, lists, dicts,
sets, booleans, and None.

Even when passing in Unicode, the resulting Str types parsed are 'str' in Python 2.
I don't now how to set 'unicode_literals' on parse -> Str is explicitly converted.

functions.name: setup._check_convert_version
documentation: Create a PEP 386 pseudo-format conformant string from tuple tup.

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.split
documentation:
split the full package name in list of compontents traditionally
done by setuptools.find_packages. This routine skips any directories
with __init__.py, for which the name starts with "_" or ".", or the
__init__.py contains package data (indicating a subpackage)

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.namespace_directories
documentation:
return list of directories where the namespace should be created /
can be found

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.entry_points
documentation:
normally called without explicit script_name and package name
the default console_scripts entry depends on the existence of __main__.py:
if that file exists then the function main() in there is used, otherwise
the in __init__.py.

the _package_data entry_points key/value pair can be explicitly specified
including a "=" character. If the entry is True or 1 the
scriptname is the last part of the full package path (split on '.')
if the ep entry is a simple string without "=", that is assumed to be
the name of the script.

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.license
documentation: return the license field from _package_data, None means MIT

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.classifiers
documentation:
this needs more intelligence, probably splitting the classifiers from _pkg_data
and only adding defaults when no explicit entries were provided.
Add explicit Python versions in sync with tox.env generation based on python_requires?

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.install_requires
documentation: list of packages required for installation

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.install_pre
documentation: list of packages required for installation

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager._analyse_packages
documentation:
gather from configuration, names starting with * need
to be installed explicitly as they are not on PyPI
install_requires should be  dict, with keys 'any', 'py27' etc
or a list (which is as if only 'any' was defined

ToDo: update with: pep508 conditional dependencies

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.extras_require
documentation:
dict of conditions -> extra packages informaton required for installation
as of setuptools 33 doing `package ; python_version<=2.7' in install_requires
still doesn't work

https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0508/
https://wheel.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html#defining-conditional-dependencies
https://hynek.me/articles/conditional-python-dependencies/

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.ext_modules
documentation:
Check if all modules specified in the value for 'ext_modules' can be build.
That value (if not None) is a list of dicts with 'name', 'src', 'lib'
Optional 'test' can be used to make sure trying to compile will work on the host

creates and return the external modules as Extensions, unless that
is not necessary at all for the action (like --version)

test existence of compiler by using export CC=nonexistent; export CXX=nonexistent

methods.name: setup.NameSpacePackager.wheel
documentation:
temporary add setup.cfg if creating a wheel to include LICENSE file
https://bitbucket.org/pypa/wheel/issues/47

methods.name: setup.TmpFiles.bdist_wheel
documentation: pyproject doesn't allow for universal, so use setup.cfg if necessary

methods.name: setup.TmpFiles.py_project
